summary: Matched a batch of fighter animation and related fighter/item/collision functions, especially in ftanim.c and ft_07C6.c. The PR replaced multiple placeholder declarations with typed implementations, retyped fighter data fields used by animation blending and collision, reshaped ftData_UnkCallbackPairs0 into a struct array, added Kirby hat callback plumbing, and marked melee/ft/ft_07C6.c as Matching in configure.py. The decomp.dev bot reported 15 new matches, +3028 matched bytes, and ft_07C6 reaching 100% for three listed functions.
what_changed: Changed configure.py so Object(NonMatching, "melee/ft/ft_07C6.c") became Object(Matching, "melee/ft/ft_07C6.c").
Implemented ftAnim_8006F994, ftAnim_8006FA58, ftAnim_8006FB88, ftAnim_800704F0, ftAnim_8007077C, ftAnim_800707B0, ftAnim_ApplyPartAnim, and ftAnim_80070FB4 in src/melee/ft/ftanim.c.
Implemented ft_8007C630, ft_8007C6DC, and ft_8007C77C in src/melee/ft/ft_07C6.c, including setup/update of Fighter.x1614 data and item collision/coin pickup handling against It_Kind_Unk4 items.
Implemented Kirby functions ftKb_Init_UnkCallbackPairs0_0 and ftKb_SpecialN_800F0FC0, using Kirby hat state, HSD_ObjAlloc, ftParts_8007487C, ftAnim_80070200, and ftAnim_800705E0.

Changed ftData_UnkCallbackPairs0 from a two-dimensional HSD_GObjEvent table into an array of structs with x0 as HSD_GObjEvent and x4 as a callback taking Fighter_GObj*, int, float frame; ftanim now dispatches .x0 and .x4 explicitly.

Expanded fighter data typing in ft/types.h: added struct ftData_x1C for partial animation data, struct ftData_x38 for x1614 setup data, struct Fighter_x8B0_t[5] for part animation state, and struct Fighter_x1614_t[2] for collision-related vectors.
Changed FighterPartsTable from an anonymous typedef style to a named struct with a forward declaration in ft/forward.h.
Added itUnk4_ItemVars to itCharItems.h and placed it in Item.xDD4_itemVar as unk4, exposing xDEC bitfields and xDF0 matrix data needed by ft_8007C77C.

Retyped lbColl_80007B78 to return bool and accept struct Fighter_x1614_t* instead of a raw Mtx, returning lbColl_800067F8 directly; updated lbcollision.h and also typed lbColl_8000A1A8's first argument as struct Fighter_x1614_t*.

Adjusted Kirby fighter variable and attribute layouts: hat.x1C became u32, added x28 and nested KirbyFV_x44_t x44, and changed ftKb_DatAttrs static assert from 0x434 to 0x424 after removing listed Final Cutter fields from that struct.
smart_moves: Used stronger struct typing to make previously raw memory accesses express the actual data flow needed for matching, especially Fighter.x8B0, Fighter.x1614_t, ftData_x1C, ftData_x38, and itUnk4_ItemVars.

Retyped callback-table layout to match how ftanim uses callbacks: one callback for animation reset/update with Fighter_GObj*, and one texture/costume callback with Fighter_GObj*, part index, and frame.
Converted lbColl_80007B78 from void/raw-matrix parameters to a bool-returning function using Fighter_x1614_t fields, allowing ft_8007C77C to naturally branch on the collision result.

Kept function prototypes synchronized across headers as implementations became known, replacing UNK_PARAMS and HSD_GObj* with more specific Fighter_GObj*, KirbyHatStruct*, u8*, int, float, and DObjList-related types.
Used the decomp project’s existing address-suffixed naming while improving internal struct names only where evidence existed, avoiding speculative semantic names for still-unknown fields.
Preserved matching-sensitive idioms visible in the diff, such as PAD_STACK(4), explicit signed ARRAY_SIZE casts in some loops, temporary variables, and an inline helper for TObj animation.
decomp_lessons: When matching animation code, expose the fighter’s per-part animation state as a real array struct rather than a filler blob; ftAnim_8007077C, ftAnim_800707B0, ftAnim_ApplyPartAnim, and ftAnim_80070FB4 all depend on coherent x8B0 fields.
Callback tables may be better represented as arrays of small structs rather than multi-dimensional arrays when different callback slots have different signatures.

Matching related systems often requires typing cross-module data first: ft_07C6 depended on fighter data structs, item vars, collision function signatures, player coin APIs, effect spawning, and audio calls.
Retyping a helper return value from UNK_RET/void to bool can unlock natural branch structure in callers, as with lbColl_80007B78 inside the item collision loop.

Struct-size assertions should be updated only when the surrounding layout evidence changes; this PR changed ftKb_DatAttrs from 0x434 to 0x424 after removing specific Final Cutter fields from that struct.
Forward declarations are preferable when headers only need pointer-level knowledge; this PR added FighterPartsTable and Fighter_x1614_t forwards rather than forcing broader includes.
A file can become fully Matching as a result of a focused cluster of function matches plus the supporting header/schema changes; ft_07C6.c was moved to Matching after its remaining functions matched.
naming_conventions: Preserved address-based unknown function names such as ftAnim_800704F0, ft_8007C77C, ftKb_SpecialN_800F0FC0, and lbColl_80007B78.
Used subsystem prefixes consistently: ftAnim_ for fighter animation, ftKb_ for Kirby, ftData_ for fighter data, lbColl_ for collision library, itUnk4_ for item vars.
Used typed gobj aliases where evidence supported them: Fighter_GObj* replaced HSD_GObj* in ft_07C6.h and Kirby/ftanim prototypes.
Introduced field-preserving temporary struct names for unknown layouts, such as Fighter_x8B0_t, Fighter_x1614_t, ftData_x1C, ftData_x38, KirbyFV_x44_t, and itUnk4_ItemVars.
Kept unknown field names with offsets or x-prefixed names such as x8B0, x1614, xDEC_b0, xDEC_b1, xDF0, x14, x20, and x44 rather than over-naming them.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(4) in ftKb_SpecialN_800F0FC0, indicating stack-shape preservation was needed for the match.
Used explicit local temporaries and ordering in ft_8007C77C, including next = HSD_GObjGetNext(cur_gobj) before mutating/deleting the current item.
Used a static inline tobjAnim wrapper around HSD_AObjReqAnim and HSD_TObjAnim, likely to preserve call structure and reuse in ftAnim_800704F0.
Used signed casts around ARRAY_SIZE in several loops, matching compiler comparison behavior for arrays such as fp->x1614 and fp->x8B0.
Encoded skipped fighter parts through ftParts_8007506C loops while traversing joint trees, matching the animation skeleton-to-parts mapping logic.
Matched animation blending by computing var_f28/var_f29 from x8/xC/x4 and choosing between lb_8000C490 and lbCopyJObjSRT depending on blend weight.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported matched code increased to 40.37% (+0.08%, +3028 bytes) and linked code increased to 26.09% (+0.02%, +768 bytes).

The bot listed 15 new matches, including ft_8007C77C, ftAnim_8006FB88, ftAnim_800707B0, ftAnim_8006FA58, ftAnim_800704F0, ftKb_SpecialN_800F0FC0, ftAnim_8006F994, ftAnim_ApplyPartAnim, ft_8007C630, ft_8007C6DC, ftKb_Init_UnkCallbackPairs0_0, ftAnim_8007077C, ftAnim_80070FB4, ftKb_Init_OnLoad, and ftKb_Init_LoadSpecialAttrs.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename Fighter_x8B0_t or Fighter_x1614_t fields to semantic names.
Search for follow-up changes to ftData_UnkCallbackPairs0 to confirm callback-slot meanings x0 and x4.
Search later PRs involving KirbyFV_x44_t, ftAnim_80070200, and ftAnim_800705E0 to refine Kirby hat animation data naming.
Check later lbcollision work for further cleanup of lbColl_80007B78 casts and Fighter_x1614_t collision capsule semantics.
Look for PRs that revisit ftKb_DatAttrs size 0x424 and the removed Final Cutter fields.
